name: qsv-pivotp
description: Pivot CSV data. Features "smart" aggregation auto-selection based on data type & stats.

args.name: on-cols
args.description: The column(s) to pivot on (creates new columns). When omitted, pivotp runs in group-by mode.

args.name: input
args.description: The input CSV file. The file must have headers. If the file has a pschema.json file, it will be used to inform the pivot operation unless --infer-len is explicitly set to a value other than the default of 10,000 rows. Stdin is not supported.

options.description: The aggregation function to use: first - First value encountered last - Last value encountered sum - Sum of values min - Minimum value max - Maximum value mean - Average value median - Median value len - Count of values item - Get single value from group. Raises error if there are multiple values. smart - use value column data type & statistics to pick an aggregation. Always uses type, cardinality, sparsity, CV, sign distribution (n_negative/n_positive), and sort_order from streaming stats. When the stats cache includes non-streaming stats (from a prior `stats --everything` or `stats --mode --quartiles`), also uses skewness and mode_count. When moarstats has been run, also leverages outlier profile, Pearson skewness, MAD/stddev ratio, median/mean ratio, and quartile coefficient of dispersion for smarter selection. With moarstats --advanced, also uses kurtosis, bimodality, entropy and Gini coefficient. For Date/DateTime values, checks sparsity and sort order. Will only work if there is one value column, otherwise it falls back to `first`
The separator in generated column names in case of multiple --values columns. (pivot mode only; ignored in group-by mode)
Use comma as decimal separator when READING the input. Note that you will need to specify an alternate --delimiter.
The field delimiter for reading/writing CSV data. Must be a single character. (default: ,)
Append a grand total row summing all numeric non-index columns. The first index column will contain "Grand <total-label>".
Skip rows that can't be parsed.

The column(s) to use as the index (row labels). Specify multiple columns by separating them with a comma. The output will have one row for each unique combination of the index's values. If None, all remaining columns not specified on --on and --values will be used. At least one of --index and --values must be specified. Required in group-by mode.
Number of rows to scan when inferring schema. Set to 0 to scan entire file.
Maintain output order: preserve input column order in pivot mode, and preserve group/row order in group-by mode.
Write output to <file> instead of stdout.
Sort the transposed columns by name. (pivot mode only)
Insert subtotal rows after each group in the first index column. The second index column will contain the total label. Requires 2+ index columns. (pivot mode only)
When set, will attempt to parse columns as dates.
Validate a pivot by checking the pivot column(s)' cardinality. (pivot mode only)

The column(s) containing values to aggregate. If an aggregation is specified, these are the values on which the aggregation will be computed. If None, all remaining columns not specified on --on and --index will be used. At least one of --index and --values must be specified. In group-by mode, if omitted, a single "count" column is produced.
